name: Entrambasaguas, Río Aguanaz, Radar Solórzano – circular route
name_original: Entrambasaguas, Río Aguanaz, Radar Solórzano – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Entrambasaguas navigate a landscape characterized by rolling reliefs, river valleys, and moderately high mountains. The region, situated in Cantabria, Spain, features a blend of traditional rural areas, extensive woodlands, and cow pastures. Cyclists encounter varied topography, from the scenic Miera River valleys to ascents like Mount Vizmaya, offering diverse experiences for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Entrambasaguas The most popular road cycling route is Entrambasaguas, Río Aguanaz, Radar Solórzano – circular route, a 28.6 miles (46.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the scenic Río Aguanaz and offers varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alto de La Encina – Puerto de Alisas loop from Puente Agüero, a difficult 47.8 miles (76.9 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, traversing mountainous terrain with rewarding views. Local road cyclists also love the Summit of Fuente Las Varas – Campo La Cruz loop from Solares, a 39.6 miles (63.7 km) trail leading through varied landscapes with substantial climbs, often completed in about 3 hours 48 minutes. Road cycling around Entrambasaguas is defined by rolling hills, scenic river valleys, and lush woodlands. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Entrambasaguas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Entrambasaguas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Entrambasaguas?
A: There are nearly 20 road cycling routes around Entrambasaguas, catering to various preferences. You'll find options ranging from easy to difficult, ensuring there's a ride for every skill level.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Entrambasaguas?
A: Entrambasaguas offers a diverse landscape for road cycling, characterized by rolling reliefs, scenic river valleys like the Miera and Aguanaz, and moderately high mountains such as Mount Vizmaya. You'll encounter a mix of traditional rural areas, extensive woodlands, and cow pastures, providing varied scenery and challenges.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Entrambasaguas?
A: Yes, Entrambasaguas has road cycling routes suitable for beginners. While many routes offer moderate to difficult challenges, there is at least one easy route available, allowing new cyclists to enjoy the region's beauty at a comfortable pace.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Entrambasaguas?
A: The road cycling routes in Entrambasaguas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Over 500 road cyclists have explored the varied terrain, often praising the picturesque natural beauty and the diverse experiences offered by the region's routes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Entrambasaguas?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Entrambasaguas are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004570712/entrambasaguas-rio-aguanaz-radar-solorzano-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrambasaguas, Río Aguanaz, Radar Solórzano – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39561670" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of Fuente Las Varas – Campo La Cruz loop from Solares&lt;/a&gt; offers a more challenging circular experience.

Q: Can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Absolutely. Entrambasaguas and its surroundings offer several difficult road cycling routes with significant elevation gain, perfect for endurance and climbing practice. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15059461" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Encina – Puerto de Alisas loop from Puente Agüero&lt;/a&gt; feature substantial climbs and rewarding views across mountainous terrain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes in Entrambasaguas?
A: Road cycling routes in Entrambasaguas often pass through scenic river valleys, lush woodlands, and offer views of rolling hills and moderately high mountains. You might encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2092775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Alisas&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1605149" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo La Cruz&lt;/a&gt; mountain passes, and even glimpse the rugged limestone massif of Mount Vizmaya. The region also has a rich history, with remnants of 19th-century iron mining and historical sites.

Q: Is it possible to combine road cycling with a visit to Cabárceno Nature Park?
A: Yes, Cabárceno Nature Park is located nearby and offers a unique experience. While the park is primarily for wildlife viewing, it features over 20 km of paved roads that can be explored by e-bike, allowing you to combine a different kind of cycling with observing animals in semi-freedom. This could be a great addition to your road cycling trip in the region.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Entrambasaguas?
A: Cantabria, where Entrambasaguas is located, generally experiences a mild climate. Spring and autumn are often ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can also be good, though it might be warmer. It's always advisable to check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Entrambasaguas?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation, there are easier road cycling options that families might enjoy. These routes typically follow flatter sections along river valleys or through rural areas. It's recommended to check the difficulty grade and elevation profiles of specific routes to ensure they are suitable for all family members.

Q: What are the options for parking when road cycling in Entrambasaguas?
A: Parking availability for road cyclists in Entrambasaguas and surrounding areas will vary depending on your chosen starting point. Many routes begin in or near towns like Solares or Entrambasaguas itself, where public parking is generally available. For specific routes, it's best to check the route details on komoot for recommended starting points and parking information.

Q: Can I use public transport to access road cycling routes in Entrambasaguas?
A: Public transport options for accessing road cycling routes in Entrambasaguas may be limited, especially for carrying bikes. While local buses might serve some towns, their capacity for bicycles can vary. It's advisable to research specific bus lines or train services in Cantabria if you plan to use public transport, as policies for bikes can differ.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation for cyclists in Entrambasaguas?
A: Entrambasaguas and the surrounding towns offer various amenities for visitors. You can find cafes and local establishments for refreshments, as well as accommodation options ranging from guesthouses to hotels. These can be found in the main villages along or near popular cycling routes, providing convenient stops during or after your ride.
